stories.title: Target Cat & Jack Children's Sandals Recalled — 211,000 Pairs, Choking Hazard

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
⚠️ RECALL: Target Cat & Jack tan sandals are being recalled — 211,000 pairs, sizes 5T–12T. Decorative pearls can fall off and pose a choking hazard. Stop use now. Call 800-591-3869 for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Target recalled Cat & Jack tan sandals this week — 211,000 pairs, sizes 5T–12T. Pearl decorations can fall off and pose a choking risk. 23 reports, no injuries. Stop use. Call Target: 800-591-3869.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
If you bought a pair of tan Cat & Jack sandals from Target for your child this year, we want you to know about this recall right away.

Target is recalling about 211,000 pairs of Cat & Jack tan sandals — sizes 5T through 12T — because the decorative plastic pearls on the straps can fall off and become a choking hazard. The CPSC announcement went out Wednesday, July 16. These sandals were sold at Target stores nationwide and on target.com from January through May 2026 for around $20.

There have been 23 reports of the pearls detaching. No injuries have been reported, but the CPSC classified the risk as serious enough that parents should stop letting their kids wear these sandals immediately.

Here's what to do: Stop using the sandals and call Target at 800-591-3869 to get a full refund. You don't need the receipt — just the sandals (or even just the order confirmation if you bought online).

Tag a friend who might have these at home. It only takes one moment.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 8:00 AM MT

```
RECALL ALERT | Target Cat & Jack tan sandals, sizes 5T–12T. Stop use. Call 800-591-3869 for a full refund. Pearl decorations can detach and pose a choking hazard.
```

article.headline: Target Recalls Cat & Jack Kids' Sandals — 211,000 Pairs, Choking Hazard from Pearl Decorations

stories.title: El Paso County Fair — Final Day Is Today

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
📣 LAST DAY: The El Paso County Fair closes TODAY in Calhan. Rodeo, demo derbies, carnival rides, livestock shows. The 121st annual fair — one last chance to go before it wraps for 2026.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Final day of the El Paso County Fair in Calhan. Auto races, rodeo, carnival rides, live entertainment — great for families with kids of all ages. See elpasocountyfair.com for today's full schedule.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
One more chance to catch the El Paso County Fair — TODAY is the last day.

The 121st annual El Paso County Fair wraps up tonight in Calhan, about 30 minutes east of Colorado Springs. If your family has been meaning to go and kept putting it off, today is it. They were aiming to draw 25,000 visitors this year, which gives you a sense of the scale of the event.

What's waiting for you: a full rodeo, auto races and demolition derbies, carnival rides, live entertainment, and livestock shows. It's the kind of fair that doesn't feel like anything else you can do on a Saturday around here — the combination of rodeo culture and carnival energy is something kids remember.

Calhan is about 35 minutes from downtown Colorado Springs via Highway 24. Check elpasocountyfair.com for today's schedule and admission details before you head out.

Are you going today, or did you catch it earlier in the week?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 10:00 AM MT

```
LAST DAY | 121st Annual El Paso County Fair in Calhan. Rodeo, rides, demos, and livestock shows. Today, July 18, is the final day.
```

article.headline: Today Is Your Last Chance: 121st Annual El Paso County Fair Closes Tonight in Calhan

stories.title: Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo — Final Day Today

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
🤠 TODAY ONLY: Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo at Norris Penrose Event Center. Matinee at 12:30 PM, evening at 7:30 PM. Mutton-bustin' for the little ones, bull riding for the whole family. Grounds open at 10 AM.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
There are two chances left to catch the Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo — and they're both today.

The 89th annual rodeo runs its final day at Norris Penrose Event Center with a matinee at 12:30 PM and an evening show at 7:30 PM. Grounds open at 10 AM for the matinee, so families can get there early and soak it all in before the action starts.

This rodeo has been a Colorado Springs tradition since 1937, and it supports military families — so there's a community dimension that makes it feel a little different from a typical rodeo. Kids tend to love it, and mutton-bustin' is always a highlight for the younger crowd.

If you've never taken your kids to the rodeo, this is a genuinely great first experience. It's one of those quintessential Colorado things that people from here grow up with.

Have your kids ever done mutton-bustin'? We'd love to hear your stories.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 12:00 PM MT

```
FINAL DAY | Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo at Norris Penrose. Matinee 12:30 PM, evening 7:30 PM. Grounds open 10 AM. A COS tradition since 1937.
```

article.headline: Last Chance for the Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo — Two Performances Today at Norris Penrose

stories.title: Colorado SUN Bucks — $120 Per Child for Summer Groceries, Apply by Aug. 25

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
💰 Colorado families: SUN Bucks puts $120 on an EBT card per child for summer groceries. Most kids are auto-enrolled — but some families need to apply by Aug. 25. Check cdhs.colorado.gov/summer-ebt or call 800-536-5298.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Did you know Colorado is putting $120 per child on EBT cards this summer for groceries? It's called SUN Bucks, and a lot of families who qualify may not have heard about it yet.

The Colorado Summer EBT program gives one-time $120 per eligible school-age child, loaded onto an EBT card that works at grocery stores and farmers markets. Most eligible kids were automatically enrolled — but if your family wasn't, you have until August 25, 2026 to apply.

Who qualifies? Children who attend a school with the National School Lunch or Breakfast Program, where family income is up to 185% of the federal poverty level (roughly $59,000 for a family of four). Families already on SNAP, TANF, or Medicaid are automatically eligible.

To check your enrollment status or apply, visit cdhs.colorado.gov/summer-ebt or call 800-536-5298. If you have more than one kid who qualifies, it's $120 per child.

Share this with someone who might benefit — a lot of families don't know this exists.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 3:00 PM MT

```
SUN BUCKS | $120 per child for summer groceries via EBT. Apply by Aug. 25 at cdhs.colorado.gov/summer-ebt or call 800-536-5298.
```

article.headline: $120 in Free Summer Groceries Per Child: How COS Families Can Claim Colorado's SUN Bucks

image_concepts.label: Image

stories.title: Free Summer Meals for Kids Across Colorado Springs

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
🍎 Free meals for COS kids this summer: D11 is serving lunch and snacks to any child 18 and under. Find a site near you at KidsFoodFinder.org — just enter your ZIP code. No registration, no income check.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
```
Summer meals for kids in Colorado Springs don't require registration, income verification, or paperwork. You just show up.

D11 is providing free meals to any child 18 and under in Colorado Springs this summer — regardless of which school district they attend. Across the state, there are more than 600 meal sites open, and the Colorado Department of Education partnered with KidsFoodFinder.org to make finding them as simple as possible. Enter your ZIP code on KidsFoodFinder.org and you'll see nearby sites with addresses, days, and hours.

This is a USDA Summer Food Service Program, which means the funding is federal and the rules are consistent: any child 18 and under can walk in and get a free meal. There's no income check, no district requirement, and no need to sign up in advance.

If you've been stretching groceries this summer, this is one of those programs worth knowing about. And if you don't need it yourself, there's likely a family in your neighborhood who does.

Do you know of a COS family who could use this? Tag them below.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption — 5:00 PM MT

```
FREE SUMMER MEALS | Any COS kid 18 and under can get a free meal this summer — no registration needed. Find your nearest site at KidsFoodFinder.org.
```

article.headline: Free Lunch for Every Kid This Summer: How to Find a Site Near You in Colorado Springs
